title: Emergent electric fields driven by phonon-coupled skyrmion resonances
abstract: We develop a coarse-grained theoretical description of the macroscopic emergent electric field generated by phonon-coupled lattice deformations in the breathing and rotational dynamics of a skyrmion lattice under microwave excitation. The analysis identifies the symmetry and dynamical conditions that yield rectified (dc) and oscillating (ac) electric fields, even in the absence of net translational motion of the skyrmion lattice, particularly in the dilute-lattice limit. Using experimentally measurable skyrmion profile parameters such as the equilibrium radius, domain-wall width, and dynamical resonance frequency of skyrmion lattice, the model further enables identification of harmonic components contributing to the observed macroscopic electrodynamic response in the long-wavelength phonon limit ($q \to 0$) and at finite phonon frequency, providing a unified framework for phonon-driven spin-charge-lattice coupling in topological magnets.
summary: This paper provides a theoretical framework to understand how lattice vibrations (phonons) can drive electric fields in skyrmion lattices. It shows that internal skyrmion dynamics like breathing and rotation can generate measurable electrical signals even without moving the entire lattice. This is significant for developing new ways to control and detect topological magnetic textures using light or microwaves.
main_problem: The paper aims to describe the generation of macroscopic emergent electric fields resulting from the coupling between phonons and the breathing/rotational dynamics of a skyrmion lattice under microwave excitation.
main_result: The study identifies the specific symmetry and dynamical conditions required to produce both rectified (dc) and oscillating (ac) electric fields, even without net translational motion of the lattice.
method: The authors develop a coarse-grained theoretical description and a unified framework for analyzing spin-charge-lattice coupling in the long-wavelength phonon limit.
model_or_system: The system consists of a skyrmion lattice in topological magnets subject to phonon-coupled lattice deformations and microwave excitation.
key_observables: Macroscopic emergent electric fields (dc and ac components), skyrmion profile parameters, and harmonic components of the electrodynamic response.
important_parameters: Equilibrium radius, domain-wall width, dynamical resonance frequency, long-wavelength phonon limit (q -> 0), and finite phonon frequency.
main_assumptions: The analysis focuses on the dilute-lattice limit and the long-wavelength phonon limit.
paper_structure: The paper presents a theoretical development of a coarse-grained model, followed by an analysis of symmetry and dynamical conditions for electric field generation, and concludes with the identification of harmonic components in the electrodynamic response.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
